Name: adaptive_quantization_kernel_optimization
Title: Adaptive Quantization and Kernel Optimization for Enhanced Inference Performance
Short Hypothesis: Integrating adaptive quantization strategies with real-time kernel-level profiling can significantly improve inference speed and efficiency in large language models and vision transformers.
Related Work: While existing works like SliM-LLM and MicroMix focus on salience-driven and hardware-specific quantization strategies, they generally do not utilize real-time profiling to adapt quantization levels dynamically during inference. This proposal advances the field by introducing a method that combines adaptive quantization with kernel-level optimizations based on live profiling metrics.
Abstract: As large language models (LLMs) and vision transformers (ViTs) become increasingly prevalent, the need for efficient inference techniques is paramount. This proposal introduces a novel framework that integrates adaptive quantization strategies with real-time kernel-level profiling to achieve significant improvements in inference speed and efficiency. By dynamically adjusting bit-width configurations based on live profiling data—such as achieved bandwidth, occupancy, and latency metrics—this approach ensures that models operate at optimal precision levels for the current workload. We will evaluate the proposed method on commodity GPUs, measuring wall-clock latency, tokens-per-second throughput, and memory utilization across various model architectures. The results are expected to demonstrate substantial speedups compared to static quantization methods, paving the way for more responsive and efficient deployment of AI models in real-world applications.
Experiments: 1. Implement a baseline adaptive quantization method that dynamically adjusts bit-widths based on live profiling data.
2. Profile existing mixed-precision quantization approaches under fixed workloads to establish performance benchmarks.
3. Conduct A/B testing by measuring p50/p99 latency and tokens-per-second throughput for models using static vs. adaptive quantization.
4. Analyze the impact of different kernel configurations on overall performance and identify hardware-specific optimizations that can be leveraged.
5. Use nvprof/Nsight for detailed profiling of memory bandwidth utilization and kernel occupancy.
Evaluation metrics will include wall-clock latency, throughput under varying batch sizes, and energy consumption per inference.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The potential complexity of integrating adaptive quantization with existing kernel optimizations may lead to unforeseen performance bottlenecks.
Real-time profiling may introduce additional overhead that could negate some benefits, particularly in low-latency scenarios.
Dependence on specific hardware configurations may limit generalizability across different GPU architectures.
